heading: Formula grants to States
label: 10406 Formula grants to States

children.text: to prevent incidents of family violence, domestic violence, and dating violence;
to provide immediate shelter, supportive services, and access to community-based programs for victims of family violence, domestic violence, or dating violence, and their dependents; and

to provide specialized services for children exposed to family violence, domestic violence, or dating violence, underserved populations, and victims who are members of racial and ethnic minority populations.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: 1 Administrative costs
children.text: Each State may use not more than 5 percent of the grant funds for State administrative costs.

children.heading: Subgrants to eligible entities
children.label: 2 Subgrants to eligible entities
children.text: The State shall use the remainder of the grant funds to make subgrants to eligible entities for approved purposes as described in .section 10408 of this title

children.heading: Approved activities
children.label: 1 Approved activities
children.text: In carrying out the activities under this chapter, grantees and subgrantees may collaborate with and provide information to Federal, State, local, and tribal public officials and agencies, in accordance with limitations on disclosure of confidential or private information as described in paragraph (5), to develop and implement policies to reduce or eliminate family violence, domestic violence, and dating violence.

children.heading: Application of civil rights provisions
children.label: A Application of civil rights provisions
children.text: For the purpose of applying the prohibitions against discrimination on the basis of age under the Age Discrimination Act of 1975 ( et seq.), on the basis of disability under section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (), on the basis of sex under title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 ( et seq.), or on the basis of race, color, or national origin under title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.), programs and activities funded in whole or in part with funds made available under this chapter are considered to be programs and activities receiving Federal financial assistance.42 U.S.C. 610129 U.S.C. 79420 U.S.C. 168142 U.S.C. 2000d

children.heading: Prohibition on discrimination on basis of sex, religion
children.label: B Prohibition on discrimination on basis of sex, religion

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No person shall on the ground of sex or religion be excluded from participation in, be denied the benefits of, or be subject to discrimination under, any program or activity funded in whole or in part with funds made available under this chapter. Nothing in this chapter shall require any such program or activity to include any individual in any program or activity without taking into consideration that individual’s sex in those certain instances where sex is a bona fide occupational qualification or programmatic factor reasonably necessary to the normal or safe operation of that particular program or activity.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: ii Enforcement
children.text: The Secretary shall enforce the provisions of clause (i) in accordance with section 602 of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (). Section 603 of such Act () shall apply with respect to any action taken by the Secretary to enforce such clause.42 U.S.C. 2000d–142 U.S.C. 2000d–2

children.heading: Construction
children.label: iii Construction
children.text: This subparagraph shall not be construed as affecting any legal remedy provided under any other provision of law.

children.label: i
children.text: refer the matter to the Attorney General with a recommendation that an appropriate civil action be instituted;

children.label: ii
children.text: exercise the powers and functions provided by title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.), the Age Discrimination Act of 1975 ( et seq.), sections 504 and 505 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 [, 794a], or title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 ( et seq.), as may be applicable; or42 U.S.C. 2000d42 U.S.C. 610129 U.S.C. 79420 U.S.C. 1681

children.label: iii
children.text: take such other action as may be provided by law.

children.heading: Enforcement authority of Attorney General
children.label: D Enforcement authority of Attorney General
children.text: When a matter is referred to the Attorney General pursuant to subparagraph (C)(i), or whenever the Attorney General has reason to believe that a State, an Indian tribe, or an entity described in subparagraph (C) is engaged in a pattern or practice in violation of a provision of law referred to in subparagraph (A) or in violation of subparagraph (B), the Attorney General may bring a civil action in any appropriate district court of the United States for such relief as may be appropriate, including injunctive relief.

children.heading: Income eligibility standards
children.label: 3 Income eligibility standards
children.text: No income eligibility standard may be imposed upon individuals with respect to eligibility for assistance or services supported with funds appropriated to carry out this chapter. No fees may be levied for assistance or services provided with funds appropriated to carry out this chapter.

children.heading: Match
children.label: 4 Match
children.text: No grant shall be made under this section to any entity other than a State or an Indian tribe unless the entity agrees that, with respect to the costs to be incurred by the entity in carrying out the program or project for which the grant is awarded, the entity will make available (directly or through donations from public or private entities) non-Federal contributions in an amount that is not less than $1 for every $5 of Federal funds provided under the grant. The non-Federal contributions required under this paragraph may be in cash or in kind.

children.heading: Nondisclosure of confidential or private information
children.label: 5 Nondisclosure of confidential or private information

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In order to ensure the safety of adult, youth, and child victims of family violence, domestic violence, or dating violence, and their families, grantees and subgrantees under this chapter shall protect the confidentiality and privacy of such victims and their families.

children.label: i
children.text: disclose any personally identifying information collected in connection with services requested (including services utilized or denied), through grantees’ and subgrantees’ programs; or

children.label: aa
children.text: the person, except as provided in item (bb) or (cc);

children.label: bb
children.text: in the case of an unemancipated minor, the minor and the minor’s parent or guardian; or

children.label: cc
children.text: in the case of an individual with a guardian, the individual’s guardian; and

children.label: II
children.text: may not be given by the abuser or suspected abuser of the minor or individual with a guardian, or the abuser or suspected abuser of the other parent of the minor.

children.label: i
children.text: grantees and subgrantees shall make reasonable attempts to provide notice to victims affected by the release of the information; and

children.label: ii
children.text: grantees and subgrantees shall take steps necessary to protect the privacy and safety of the persons affected by the release of the information.

children.label: i
children.text: nonpersonally identifying information, in the aggregate, regarding services to their clients and demographic nonpersonally identifying information in order to comply with Federal, State, or tribal reporting, evaluation, or data collection requirements;

children.label: ii
children.text: court-generated information and law enforcement-generated information contained in secure, governmental registries for protective order enforcement purposes; and

children.label: iii
children.text: law enforcement- and prosecution-generated information necessary for law enforcement and prosecution purposes.

children.heading: Oversight
children.label: E Oversight
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall prevent the Secretary from disclosing grant activities authorized in this chapter to the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and exercising congressional oversight authority. In making all such disclosures, the Secretary shall protect the confidentiality of individuals and omit personally identifying information, including location information about individuals and shelters.

children.heading: Statutorily permitted reports of abuse or neglect
children.label: F Statutorily permitted reports of abuse or neglect
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall prohibit a grantee or subgrantee from reporting abuse and neglect, as those terms are defined by law, where mandated or expressly permitted by the State or Indian tribe involved.

children.heading: Preemption
children.label: G Preemption
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to supersede any provision of any Federal, State, tribal, or local law that provides greater protection than this paragraph for victims of family violence, domestic violence, or dating violence.

children.heading: Confidentiality of location
children.label: H Confidentiality of location
children.text: The address or location of any shelter facility assisted under this chapter that otherwise maintains a confidential location shall, except with written authorization of the person or persons responsible for the operation of such shelter, not be made public.

children.heading: Supplement not supplant
children.label: 6 Supplement not supplant
children.text: Federal funds made available to a State or Indian tribe under this chapter shall be used to supplement and not supplant other Federal, State, tribal, and local public funds expended to provide services and activities that promote the objectives of this chapter.

children.heading: Reports and evaluation
children.label: d Reports and evaluation
children.text: Each grantee shall submit an annual performance report to the Secretary at such time as shall be reasonably required by the Secretary. Such performance report shall describe the grantee and subgrantee activities that have been carried out with grant funds made available under subsection (a) or , contain an evaluation of the effectiveness of such activities, and provide such additional information as the Secretary may reasonably require.section 10409 of this title
